conjecture_statement: Let $C_{\\\\text{GRS}}$ be a Generalized Reed-Solomon code over the finite field $\\\\mathbb{F}_{16}$ with parameters $[n,k,d] = [17, 3, 15]$. Let $\\\\phi: \\\\mathbb{F}_{16} \\\\to (\\\\mathbb{F}_2)^4$ be an isomorphism of $\\\\mathbb{F}_2$-vector spaces. The binary code $C_{\\\\text{bin}} = \\\\{ (\\\\phi(c_0), \\\\dots, \\phi(c_{16})) \\\\mid (c_0, \\\\dots, c_{16}) \\\\in C_{\\\\text{GRS}} \\\\} \\\\subset (\\\\mathbb{F}_2)^{68}$ has minimum Hamming distance $d(C_{\\\\text{bin}}) = 15$.
lean4_sketch:
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Card\
import Mathlib.FieldTheory.Finite.Basic\
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Basis\
import Mathlib.Code.LinearCode\
\
-- Conjectured setup\
namespace Bklc6815\
\
-- Let F be the finite field with 16 elements.\
variable (F : Type) [Field F] [Fintype F] (hF : Fintype.card F = 16)\
\
-- A GRS code is a type of evaluation code.\
-- We postulate its existence and key properties as it is not yet defined in Mathlib.\
-- A full proof would require constructing evaluation codes.\
-- Parameters: n=17, k=3, over F\
\
-- We model the code as a subspace of (Fin 17 → F)\
variable (C_GRS : Subspace F (Fin 17 → F))\
\
-- Axiom 1: Dimension is 3\
variable (h_dim : Fintype.card C_GRS = (Fintype.card F) ^ 3)\
\
-- Axiom 2: It is an MDS code, so its distance is n-k+1 = 17-3+1 = 15\
